title: On the tram
desc: Strike up a chat with a stranger
setting: You're on the tram. An older man beside you comments on the weather. You can let it die or build a connection.

steps.speaker: Maa
steps.es: Sone Räge hüt, mer chunnt ja fascht nöd use.
steps.en: Such rain today, you can hardly get outside.

options.es: Ja würkli, und das im Summer! Hoffentli wird's bald besser.
options.en: Yes really, and in summer! Hopefully it'll get better soon.
options.feedback: You catch the ball and toss it back — textbook Swiss small talk.
He opened a friendly door — a half-word lets it swing shut.

options.en: I don't pay attention to the weather.
options.feedback: A small snub to a kindly opener. Weather chat is the social glue here.

steps.speaker: Maa
steps.es: Genau. Früecher hämmer im Juni no chönne grille.
steps.en: Exactly. We used to be able to barbecue in June.

options.es: Ächt? Da het sich s'Wätter scho verändert, oder?
options.en: Really? The weather has changed, hasn't it?
options.feedback: You show interest and keep it rolling — he'll enjoy this ride now.

options.es: Ich bi nöd vo da, ich weiss's nöd.
options.en: I'm not from here, I wouldn't know.
options.feedback: Honest, but you can still engage — ask him what it used to be like.
A bit of a conversation-stopper — a small question keeps it alive.

steps.speaker: Maa
steps.es: Sind Sie nöd vo da? Mer hört's fascht gar nöd.
steps.en: You're not from here? You can hardly tell at all.

options.es: Merci! Ich läb sit es paar Jahr da und lärne fliissig.
options.en: Thank you! I've lived here for a few years and I learn diligently.
options.feedback: Receives the compliment well and shares a little — warm and confident.

options.es: Nei, us Amerika. Aber Luzärn isch jetz mis Dehei.
options.en: No, from America. But Lucerne is my home now.
options.feedback: Honest, warm, and a lovely thing for a local to hear. Perfect.
He just said it isn't! Take the compliment rather than fishing.

steps.speaker: Maa
steps.es: Da müend Sie aussteige, gäll. Schöne Tag no!
steps.en: This is your stop, isn't it. Have a nice day!

options.es: Merci, Ihne au! Het mi gfreut.
options.en: Thanks, you too! It was a pleasure.
options.feedback: Warm, complete, and very Swiss. A perfect little encounter.
Simple and friendly — exactly right for parting with a stranger.
A bit flat after a nice chat — a touch more warmth rounds it off.
